name: Montecalvo Loop from Gernika
name_original: Montecalvo – circular desde Guernica y Luno
description: This difficult 19.5-mile touring cycling route from Guernica y Luno offers rewarding views of the sea and Urdaibai estuary from Montecalvo.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Guernica Y Luno are set within the diverse landscapes of the Urdaibai Biosphere Reserve in the Basque Country. The region features a varied topography, including coastal areas, river valleys, and hills, providing a range of elevations for cyclists. The area is characterized by green spaces, waterways, and cultural sites, with the Urdaibai estuary offering dynamic views. Best touring cycling routes around Guernica Y Luno The most popular touring cycling route is Montecalvo Loop from Gernika, a 19.5 miles (31.3 km) trail that takes 2 hours 11 minutes to complete. This route offers vistas of the sea and the Urdaibai estuary. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Alto de Morga Loop from Guernica, a difficult 18.9 miles (30.5 km) path. It includes significant climbs with over 2,000 feet of elevation gain. Local touring cyclists also love the Guernica Mural in Gernika – Artzubi Bridge loop from Lurgorri, a 16.4 miles (26.4 km) trail leading through historical and cultural sites, often completed in about 1 hour 43 minutes. Touring cycling around Guernica Y Luno is defined by the Urdaibai Biosphere Reserve, coastal scenery, and river valleys. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging routes with significant elevation. The routes in Guernica Y Luno are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 400 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Guernica Y Luno's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Guernica Y Luno?
A: Komoot offers over 30 touring cycling routes around Guernica Y Luno. These routes cater to various skill levels, with options ranging from moderate to difficult, allowing cyclists to explore the diverse landscapes of the Urdaibai Biosphere Reserve.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes around Guernica Y Luno?
A: The region offers a varied topography, including coastal areas, river valleys, and hills. You can expect routes with significant elevation gains, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1085237961/alto-de-morga-loop-from-guernica" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Morga Loop from Guernica&lt;/a&gt; which features over 2,000 feet of climbing. While many surfaces are paved, some routes may include unpaved segments, adding to the adventure.

Q: Are there routes suitable for beginners or families in Guernica Y Luno?
A: While many routes in Guernica Y Luno are rated as difficult, there are a few moderate options available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33787563" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guernica Mural in Gernika – Artzubi Bridge loop from Lurgorri&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 16.4-mile trail that leads through historical sites, offering a less strenuous ride suitable for those looking for a more relaxed experience.

Q: What natural features can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: Cycling in Guernica Y Luno offers stunning natural scenery, particularly within the Urdaibai Biosphere Reserve. You can enjoy impressive vistas of the sea and the Urdaibai estuary, as seen on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1085220146/montecalvo-loop-from-gernika" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montecalvo Loop from Gernika&lt;/a&gt;. Other notable natural features include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6628930" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Oma Forest&lt;/a&gt; (Painted Forest) and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5269209" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views over the Urdaibai marsh&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks to explore on a touring cycling trip?
A: Absolutely. Guernica Y Luno is rich in history and culture. You can cycle past the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3921343" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Artzubi Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, a beautiful 16th-century Romanesque bridge, or explore the area around the Guernica Mural, which commemorates the 1937 bombing. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1050528" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Tree of Gernika (Gernikako Arbola)&lt;/a&gt; and the Casa de Juntas are also significant historical sites representing Basque autonomy.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Guernica Y Luno?
A: The Basque Country generally experiences mild temperatures, making touring cycling enjoyable for much of the year. Spring and autumn often provide pleasant weather with fewer crowds. Summer can be warm, especially inland, while winters are typically mild but can be rainy. Always check the local forecast before heading out.

Q: Are there circular touring cycling routes in the region?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes around Guernica Y Luno are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1085220146/montecalvo-loop-from-gernika" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montecalvo Loop from Gernika&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1085237961/alto-de-morga-loop-from-guernica" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Morga Loop from Guernica&lt;/a&gt;, which offer convenient circular options for exploration.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Guernica Y Luno?
A: The touring cycling routes in Guernica Y Luno are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 10 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, the stunning coastal views, and the rich cultural sites encountered along the way.

Q: Is public transport available to reach the touring cycling routes?
A: Guernica Y Luno is well-connected within the Basque Country. Public transport options, including local buses and trains, can help you reach the town center, which serves as a starting point for many routes. It's advisable to check specific schedules and bicycle transport policies with local operators.

Q: Where can I park my car when starting a touring cycling route?
A: In Guernica Y Luno, parking is generally available in and around the town center. Many routes begin from accessible points within the town, where public parking facilities can be found. For routes starting further afield, look for designated parking areas near trailheads or local attractions.

Q: Are there places to eat or drink along the touring cycling routes?
A: The region offers numerous opportunities to refuel. Guernica Y Luno itself has a variety of cafes, restaurants, and bars. Many routes pass through or near smaller villages and towns within the Urdaibai Biosphere Reserve, where you can find local establishments offering refreshments and traditional Basque cuisine.

Q: Are there any very long touring cycling routes for multi-day trips?
A: While many routes are suitable for day trips, some longer options exist, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38204897" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Larrabetzu Old Town – Butrón Castle loop from Lurgorri&lt;/a&gt;, which spans nearly 50 miles (79.5 km). These longer routes can be combined or extended to create multi-day touring experiences, allowing you to explore more of the Basque Country's diverse landscapes and cultural sites.

This mural reproduction of Picasso's famous painting Guernica commemorates the bombing of the city of Guernica in 1937 during the Spanish Civil War. The painting captures, through symbols, the horrors of war.

Montecalvo is located at an altitude of 381 meters, offering impressive views of the sea and Urdaibai on one side and Duranguesado on the other. The Muniketa road was the main communication route south through Durango until 1848, when the Autzagane road was improved.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start the Montecalvo circular cycling route?
faqs.answer: The starting point of the route in Guernica y Luno is conveniently located next to a parking lot, making it easy to access for cyclists arriving by car.

faqs.question: How challenging is the Montecalvo – circular from Guernica y Luno route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as a 'difficult' touring bicycle ride, requiring a very good level of fitness due to its significant elevation gain of approximately 590 meters over 31.3 kilometers. It's a challenging but rewarding experience.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this circular cycling route?
faqs.answer: The Montecalvo circular route is noted for having mostly paved surfaces, making it generally suitable for touring bicycles. Despite the overall difficulty from elevation, the surface type is consistent and well-maintained.

faqs.question: What can I see along the Montecalvo cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route offers a blend of natural beauty and historical significance. You'll pass through Guernica y Luno, where you can see the Guernica Mural and the Old Tree of Gernika. From Alto de Muniketa (Montecalvo), you'll enjoy impressive views of the sea, the Urdaibai estuary, and the Duranguesado region.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The estimated duration for completing the Montecalvo – circular from Guernica y Luno route is approximately 2 hours and 11 minutes, though this can vary based on your cycling pace and stops.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: While the trail can be enjoyed year-round, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant cycling conditions with milder temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summer can be warm, and winter might bring cooler, wetter weather.

faqs.question: Is the Montecalvo – circular from Guernica y Luno trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This route is primarily a touring bicycle trail on mostly paved surfaces. While dogs are generally allowed on public roads and paths, it's always best to keep them on a leash for their safety and the safety of others, especially given the cycling nature of the route. Consider your dog's fitness for a 31 km ride.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required to cycle this route, especially within the Urdaibai Biosphere Reserve?
faqs.answer: No specific permits or entrance fees are required to cycle the Montecalvo route. As the route passes through the Urdaibai Biosphere Reserve, please adhere to all local regulations regarding environmental protection and respect the natural surroundings.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. Notably, it covers sections of the [CIMA BI13] Monte Oiz * Gernika, [CIMA BI14] Muniketa * Zugastieta, and the Ardoaren eta arrainaren bidea (Wine and Fish Route).

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or restaurants along the Montecalvo circular cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Guernica y Luno, which offers various cafes, pubs, and restaurants. While there might not be many options directly on the more rural sections of the Montecalvo climb, you'll find plenty of amenities in the town for pre- or post-ride refreshments.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: Given its 'difficult' rating and significant elevation gain, this route is generally not recommended for families with young children or beginner cyclists. It requires a very good level of fitness and experience with challenging ascents.
